name: Pasta Without Tomato- Old
recipeIngredient: 1 ounce spaghetti, broken into halves or thirds
a pinch of ground white pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure 1 ½ to 1 ¾ cups of mixed pasta in a 2‑cup Pyrex measuring cup.
- Break about 1 ounce of spaghetti into halves or thirds and add it to the cup.
- Add enough rotini (about 2 ounces) to fill the remainder of the cup.
2. Cook the combined pasta together; the spaghetti will become over‑cooked while the starch acts as a binder.
3. In a separate pan, brown the beef over moderately high heat until no longer pink. Remove excess fat.
- Add olive oil and onions; fry, stirring, until softened and translucent.
4. Stir in garlic powder, mushrooms, parsley, and Italian seasoning. Cook until mushrooms are warmed but not limp.
5. When the pasta is done, drain it (do not rinse). Return the pasta to its cooking pot.
- In a well in the center of the pasta mixture, combine the egg and extra olive oil.
- Pour the egg mixture quickly over the pasta, stirring constantly to coat everything and gently cook the eggs.
- Season with dried basil, oregano, and pepper. Add Parmesan cheese and optional cilantro.
Serving suggestion: vegetable medley or mixed greens salad.
